You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You self-scope from the recommendation, roadmap, and opened inbox below.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level: experiment, change the strategy entirely, try alternative formulations, edit blueprint material when proving teaches you something, and ‚ÄĒ when a task calls for it ‚ÄĒ work across the several projects it spans, or create and merge projects. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the projects in scope and the shared `references/`, don't leave stray directories or scratch files behind. The Ground agent and its review subagents run after you to reconcile, check math/blueprint alignment, and tidy ‚ÄĒ so prefer making progress over being cautious.

How to work:
- Read the relevant blueprint node and proof sketch before touching Lean.
- Run the project's build/check command (or the narrowest useful Lean check) and repair failures you introduce. Don't bury obligations under new `sorry`s unless a hint allows it.
- Use the inbox to comment, close, create, and message other projects with `--author horizon` (see the horizon-inbox skill). ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, and reports in Markdown ‚ÄĒ short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. Give every item a short title and a non-empty description. Comment at each key advance, and add a concise closing comment (runs, LOC, files) before you `complete` an item. Treat `[persistent]` items as standing rules; close `[temporary]` ones once consumed.
- Record durable dead ends as memory: `horizon inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --author horizon --body "..."`, so later sessions don't repeat them.
- You may delegate to subagents when it helps (see the Subagents section): split a wide search across parallel read-only subagents, hand mechanical checks to a cheaper-model subagent, or pull references. You are free to decide whether they fit the task.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` ‚ÄĒ a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions ‚ÄĒ The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style ‚ÄĒ environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-inbox ‚ÄĒ Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox ‚ÄĒ list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- lean-check ‚ÄĒ Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors ‚ÄĒ use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag ‚ÄĒ Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) ‚ÄĒ declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects ‚ÄĒ via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch ‚ÄĒ Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git ‚ÄĒ How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace ‚ÄĒ root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents ‚ÄĒ How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work ‚ÄĒ spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Projects (this task spans several ‚ÄĒ work across all of them):
  - Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
  - Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso at SubProjects/Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso
Write scope ‚ÄĒ you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- SubProjects/Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0004/sessions/0011-horizon-T1

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint ‚ÄĒ Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end ‚ÄĒ keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug ‚ÄĒ Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run ‚ÄĒ missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors ‚ÄĒ applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor ‚ÄĒ Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier ‚ÄĒ keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber ‚ÄĒ Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever ‚ÄĒ Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] ‚ÄĒ Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task ‚ÄĒ starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper ‚ÄĒ then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.
- blueprint-reviewer [read-only] ‚ÄĒ Check that the Lean declarations match their blueprint statements and the \uses dependency edges are correct.
- diff-auditor [read-only] ‚ÄĒ Audit the project diff from the last Horizon round for sound reasoning, no stray files, and no buried sorries.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits ‚ÄĒ use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` ‚ÄĒ it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground recommendation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session; treat it as fresh guidance, not as a hard command if the live state contradicts it.
## Next

- After T2 checkpoints, run one confirming `lake build AlgebraicJacobian` from a clean single-session state ‚ÄĒ seals both the new `TangentSpaceDualNumbers` root import and T2's Cech cone; then close `I-0016`.
- `AJC.pic0av` next leaf: `Spec k[őĶ] ‚ü∂ X` ‚ÜĒ local stalk maps (Mathlib `SpecToEquivOfLocalRing`), then the H¬Ļ side ‚ÄĒ gated on `AJC.picrep` / identity-component substrate before `tangentSpaceIso` can close.

# Recommended focus
Task: T1
Title: Merge Line-Bundle comparison-iso substrate into AJC
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge,Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate*,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjInverse*

Merge the finished **comparison-iso substrate** (A.1.c.sub) from `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso` back into the main tree.

- Fold `TensorObjInverse` (`trivialisation_restrict_compat` + the terminal comparison inverse) and the DUAL / D3‚Ä≤ `TensorObjSubstrate` into `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate*`.
- Close the **5 residual shared sorries** (`TensorObjSubstrate` √ó3 + `PullbackTensorComp` √ó2).

This installs the abelian-group law on $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ via the comparison isomorphism
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$

**Done when:** AJC `lake build` is green with no new `sorry` or axioms; roadmap item `AJC.linebundle` closes.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) ńĆech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **ńĆech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC (axiom-clean). Residual 16 `sorry` are v4.31 migration-interim, mechanical.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), ńĆech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). Two open leaves: `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` (Stacks 02KG, termwise affine BC via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact). See task **T2**.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman ¬ß5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley‚ÄďRosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.

- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (K√§hler, rigidity, Abel‚ÄďJacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** K√§hler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel‚ÄďJacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).

- LBC.compinv [done/proof/high] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Terminal comparison inverse (Pic^sharp group law) - **Terminal comparison inverse (deliverable ‚ÄĒ finished).** The keystone `trivialisation_restrict_compat` and the chart-chase inverse, giving the comparison isomorphism that endows $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ with its group structure. Merges back into AJC (**T1**).

- LBC.dual [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Slice-dual transport iso (DUAL route) - **Slice-dual transport iso (DUAL route, sorry-free).**
$$\mathcal{L}^{\vee}|_{U}\;\cong\;(\mathcal{L}|_{U})^{\vee}$$
compatibly across the trivializing slices ‚ÄĒ `DualInverse`, `DualInverse/SliceTransport`.

- LBC.pullback [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Line-bundle pullback / relative Pic functor - **Line-bundle pullback / relative Pic functor (sorry-free).** `LineBundlePullback`, `RelPicFunctor`; the seed compatibility $f^{*}(\mathcal L\otimes\mathcal M)\cong f^{*}\mathcal L\otimes f^{*}\mathcal M$ on locally trivial bundles (`pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv`).

- LBC.stalktensor [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Stalk-tensor / internal-hom machinery - **Stalk-tensor / internal-hom machinery (sorry-free).** `TensorObjSubstrate/StalkTensor`, `PresheafInternalHom` ‚ÄĒ the local model for $\otimes$ and $\mathcal{H}om$ on quasi-coherent sheaves used by the comparison.


# Opened inbox
(no accepted inbox items)

# Memory
- v4.31.0 migration fix patterns (Cech + GR push-pull/pullback proofs)

Effective repairs for the **v4.31.0 defeq-transparency-wall** breaks in these projects:

- **Port from the AJC copy first.** `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.../<same file>` is often already sorry-free (e.g. `PresheafCech.lean`, `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean`). `diff` the subproject vs AJC; if they differ only in the sorry regions, copy the AJC file wholesale.
- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`** before a lemma fixes most "type mismatch under instances transparency" / stuck defeq. **Placement matters**: it must go BEFORE the docstring `/-- ‚Ä¶ -/`, never between docstring and `lemma` (that is a parse error: "unexpected token 'set_option'; expected 'lemma'"). Stacks with `set_option maxHeartbeats N in`.
- **`erw [Category.comp_id]` / `erw [Category.id_comp]`** closes residual `A ‚Čę ūĚüô = A` / `ūĚüô ‚Čę A = A` when the `ūĚüô`'s object is only defeq (e.g. `(ūĚü≠).obj M` vs `M`, or a `‚čô`-form) so `simp`/`exact Category.comp_id _` fail. `erw` matches through the wall then auto-`rfl`.
- **`rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]` ordering**: cancel the INNER hom‚Čęinv pair first so the outer pair becomes adjacent.
- **`subst h; simp <;> rfl`** for eqToHom-transport lemmas whose two sides reduce to defeq-but-not-syntactic `map = map`.
- **LSP is dead** on these full-`import Mathlib` files (server crashes / broken pipe). Iterate with `lake build <module>` and read the printed unsolved-goal state from the error output. `GlueDescent` ‚Čą 2.6 min/build; `CechHigherDirectImage` ‚Čą 8.5 min/build.
- **SOLVED (supersedes the earlier "two v4.31 sorrys remain hard" memory): both `rawPushPullMap_comp` (Cech) and `pullback_cast_compat` (GR) are now proved sorry-free.**

- **`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` `rawPushPullMap_comp`**: final transport is pure **term-mode**, no `rw` (avoids "motive not type correct"). Shape: `(congrArg (fun t => (pushforward p‚āĀ).map A_head ‚Čę t) he).trans (((Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans (congrArg (pushforward p‚āĀ).map ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans INNER))).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _))`. `he` rewrites the outer over-triangle `eqToHom` to `(pushforward p‚āĀ).map eqInner`; `Category.assoc` fixes `(x‚Čęy)‚Čęz` vs `x‚Čę(y‚Čęz)` before `INNER`; `map_comp` folds/splits.

- **`GlueDescent.lean` `pullback_cast_compat`**: proved from the associativity solved-forms. Recipe: (1) `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` BEFORE the docstring (the `pullbackComp` solved-forms unfold to the `‚čô`-object form; else v4.31 transparency wall blocks matching). (2) tagged `pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app` + `pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app` `@[reassoc]`. (3) phase-1 `simp only [pullbackComp_comp_fst_hom_app, pullback_map_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullback_map_congr_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app(_assoc), Category.assoc]` keeping congr as casts. (4) phase-2 `simp only [pullbackCongr_hom/inv_app_eqToHom, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_trans_assoc, eqToHom_refl, Category.assoc, comp_id, id_comp]` (need `eqToHom_trans_assoc` for mid-chain). Variance: `pullbackComp x y : pullback y ‚čô pullback x ‚ČÖ pullback (x‚Čęy)`.

- Iterate via `lake build <module>` reading the unsolved-goal state (LSP dead).
- **Subproject merge-back recipe (worked for T1 / Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso ‚Üí AJC; reuse for Albanese / Cech back-merges).**

- **Wholesale lift beats cherry-pick** when the subproject built green on the same toolchain+mathlib rev: replace every diverged shared file with the subproject copy, add the new files, then repair AJC-only consumers. Verify first with a decl-name superset check (regex-extract `def|lemma|...` heads, `comm -23`) and a consumer grep for the decls that moved/disappeared.
- **Check for cross-module duplicate decl names BEFORE building**: two modules both imported by the root that declare the same fully-qualified name are a fatal clash. In T1, LBC's `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` had absorbed 13 decls of AJC's `PullbackTensorComp.lean` (incl. `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`) ‚Üí the right fix was to **retire the superseded module** (its 8 unique helpers had no consumers).
- **Blueprint: port per-chapter, not per-project.** Wholesale-copy a chapter only after a comment-stripped cross-ref check: (refs of incoming chapter) ‚äÜ (its labels ‚ą™ other AJC chapters' labels), and no other AJC chapter references a disappearing label. In T1 the TOS chapter ported cleanly; LBC's restructured `RelPicFunctor`/`LineBundlePullback` chapters did NOT (6 `\cref` sites in `Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex` would dangle) ‚ÄĒ kept AJC's copies. Their reconciliation is optional future work.
- Big-file build times on this box: `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` (4592 L) + `TensorObjInverse.lean` (3424 L) + rest of the cone ‚Čą 15 min wall with warm mathlib cache.
- FBC bareBC Stage-2 patterns + scratch-iteration gotchas (session 0003-T2)

**Mate-factorization pattern (landed, reusable for other Beck-Chevalley mates):**

- `mateEquiv adj‚āĀ adj‚āā őĪ |>.app c` factors as `adj‚āā.unit.app _ ‚Čę R‚āā.map (őĪ.app _) ‚Čę R‚āā.map (H.map (adj‚āĀ.counit.app c))`. Proof: `simp [<def>, mateEquiv_apply]` then `erw [Category.id_comp, Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id]; rfl` (three defeq-blocked `ūĚüô`s + one defeq head).
- Counit of `pullback q ‚ä£ pushforward q` at open immersion `q` is iso: `IsIso.of_isIso_fac_left (Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_counit (restrictAdjunction q) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction q) c)` with `haveI` on `(restrictAdjunction q).counit` iso via `inferInstanceAs (IsIso (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso q).hom)`.
- essImage ‚Üí unit-iso: `Adjunction.isIso_unit_app_of_iso` (needs `pushforward` Full+Faithful ‚ÄĒ Mathlib instances for open immersions). Reverse `mem_essImage_of_unit_isIso`.
- **essImage of `p'_*` is cover-local** (`essImage_pushforward_of_openCover`, now in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`): site-level `restrictAdjunction` unit has plain `M.presheaf.map homOfLE` components; `isIso_iff_isIso_restrict` + `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` need NO push/pull coherence ‚ÄĒ components of the restricted map are again plain presheaf maps; match via `w''(O ‚äď w‚ĀĽ¬ĻR) = R ‚äď w''O` (`Set.image_inter_preimage`) + one `eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h)` factor. Poset-hom equalities: `congr 1` after `rw [‚Üź Functor.map_comp]` discharges by proof-irrelevance; op-level casts via `Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; Subsingleton.elim`.

**Scratch-iteration gotchas:**

- `lake env lean scratch.lean` does NOT apply lakefile `leanOptions`. Add `set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3` at scratch top or instance chains through functor categories fail mysteriously (e.g. `IsIso ((leftAdjointUniq ..).hom.app M)` composites). Destination files under `lake build` are fine.
- Mathlib-only lemmas iterate in ~3 min via a root-level scratch importing `Mathlib` (+ light project modules whose oleans exist) ‚ÄĒ 20x faster than the project file. Delete scratch before commit.
- `CechSectionIdentificationLegMid1.lean` takes ~70+ min to compile (`maxHeartbeats 0`, documented in-file); it IS in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`'s import cone (via `CechTermAcyclic ‚Üí CechSectionIdentificationBase ‚Üí ‚Ä¶ `). Full verification of Unconditional edits costs hours if its olean is stale/missing.
- Beware RACING concurrent lake builds of the same project from leftover/parallel sessions (two `lean` processes on the same file observed) ‚ÄĒ olean corruption risk, duplicated 70-min work.

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' ‚ÄĒ that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session ‚ÄĒ run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action ‚ÄĒ never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, and `## Next`; add or rename sections if another shape is clearer. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this Horizon session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

data.content:
62	\section{Group structure on the relative Picard quotient}
63	\label{sec:relpic_group}
64	
65	The Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) of any scheme is canonically an abelian group under
66	tensor product of line bundles (Stacks tag 01CR; the inverse of \([\mathcal{L}]\)
67	is $[\mathcal{L}^{-1}] = [\mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L},
68	\mathcal{O}_X)]$). The pullback map
69	\(\pi_T^* : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) of
70	\cref{def:pullback_along_projection} respects this structure: pullback of
71	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules sends \(\mathcal{O}_T\) to \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\)
72	and is multiplicative on tensor products.
73	
74	\begin{lemma}\leanok
75	[Group structure descends to the relative Picard quotient]
76	  \label{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
77	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup}
78	  \uses{def:line_bundle_on_product, def:pullback_along_projection,
79	    thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}
80	
81	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
82	  % Definition df:aPf + Definition df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard
83	  % functors are \emph{abelian-group}-valued, by construction; the relative
84	  % Picard functor is defined as a quotient \emph{group})
85	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
86	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
87	  % "The {\it absolute Picard functor} \(\Pic_X\) is the functor from  the
88	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) \(S\)-schemes \(T\) to the category of
89	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
90	  %     \(\)\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).\(\)
91	  %  [\dots]
92	  %  \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
93	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} \(\Pic_{X/S}\) is defined by
94	  %     \(\)\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).\(\)"
95	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
96	  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs (the Picard functors are abelian-group-valued, and the
97	  relative one is a quotient of abelian groups; cf.\ Stacks tag 01CR for the
98	  abelian-group structure on \(\Pic\)).}
99	  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve and let \(T\) be a
100	  \(k\)-scheme. The Picard group \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is an abelian group under
101	  tensor product of line bundles, and the pullback map
102	  \[
103	    \pi_T^* \;\colon\; \Pic(T) \;\longrightarrow\; \Pic(C \times_k T)
104	  \]
105	  of \cref{def:pullback_along_projection} is a group homomorphism. Its image
106	  \[
107	    H_T \;\;:=\;\; \pi_T^* \Pic(T)
108	  \]
109	  is therefore a subgroup of \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\), and the quotient set of
110	  \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}
111	  \[
112	    \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) \;\;=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, H_T
113	  \]

115	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \twoheadrightarrow \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) is a surjective
116	  homomorphism with kernel exactly \(H_T\).

143	\begin{proof}
144	  \uses{def:line_bundle_on_product, def:pullback_along_projection,
145	    thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined, lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
146	    lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,

152	\leanok
153	  The construction proceeds in four steps over the locally-trivial carrier
154	  \[
155	    \mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T) \;=\;
156	      \bigl\{\, M : \text{a module on } C \times_k T \;\bigm|\;
157	      M \text{ is locally trivial} \,\bigr\}
158	  \]
159	  of \cref{def:line_bundle_on_product}, on whose isomorphism classes the
160	  relative Picard quotient is taken. It is authored \emph{in parallel} against
161	  two inputs being built concurrently: the locally-trivial pullback--tensor
162	  comparison isomorphism \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv} (consumed in
163	  Step~2 to make \(\pi_T^*\) additive) and the tensor inverse
164	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} (consumed in Step~1 for group
165	  closure); both are themselves deferred targets.
166	
167	  \emph{Step 1 (abelian group on the carrier).} On isomorphism classes of the
168	  carrier \(\mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T)\), addition is the descent of the
169	  scheme-level tensor product of line bundles,
170	  \[
171	    [L] + [L'] \;:=\; [\, L \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}} L' \,],
172	  \]
173	  where \(L \otimes L'\) is the locally-trivial descent of the scheme-level
174	  tensor product (\cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}); the sum lands again in
175	  the carrier because the tensor product of two locally-trivial modules is
176	  locally trivial (\cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}). The zero
177	  element is the class \([\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}]\) of the structure sheaf,
178	  the monoidal unit, which is locally trivial. Associativity, commutativity,
179	  and left/right unitality of \(+\) descend from the scheme-level coherence
180	  isomorphisms --- the associator (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}), the
181	  braiding (\cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}), and the unitors
182	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}) --- exactly as for the absolute Picard group
183	  on tensor-invertible isomorphism classes
184	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}). The additive inverse is
185	  \[
186	    -[L] \;:=\; [\, L^{\mathrm{inv}} \,],
187	  \]
188	  where \(L^{\mathrm{inv}}\) is the witness supplied by
189	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}: it returns a \emph{locally-trivial}
190	  module together with an isomorphism
191	  \(L \otimes L^{\mathrm{inv}} \cong \mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\), so the inverse
192	  stays inside the loc-triv carrier and the group is closed under negation.
193	
194	  \emph{Step 2 (the pullback homomorphism \(\pi_T^*\)).} Pullback along the
195	  projection \(\pi_T\) gives an additive map
196	  \[
197	    \pi_T^* \;\colon\; \Pic(T) \;\longrightarrow\; \mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T),
198	  \]
199	  whose underlying assignment of line bundles is
200	  \cref{def:pullback_along_projection}. It is a homomorphism of abelian groups:
201	  it sends the unit to the unit, since
202	  \(\pi_T^*\mathcal{O}_T \cong \mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\) by the unconditional
203	  pullback--unit isomorphism (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}); and it is additive,
204	  since pullback commutes with tensor product on locally-trivial modules,
205	  \[
206	    \pi_T^*(N \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_T} N') \;\cong\;
207	      \pi_T^* N \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}} \pi_T^* N',
208	  \]
209	  by the locally-trivial comparison isomorphism
210	  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv} specialised to the projection
211	  \(\pi_T\) on the \(\mathrm{OnProduct}\) carrier. This is the exact analogue,
212	  for the geometric pullback, of the additive monoid homomorphism on Picard
213	  groups induced by a ring map respecting tensor product, and is modelled on it
214	  field-for-field. Write \(H_T := \mathrm{im}(\pi_T^*)\) for its image, a
215	  subgroup of the carrier's isomorphism-class group.
216	
217	  \emph{Step 3 (setoid reconciliation).} The set-valued quotient of
218	  \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} is taken modulo the
219	  isomorphism-class equivalence that declares two line bundles related when
220	  their classes differ by an element of \(H_T\). This relation coincides with
221	  the left-coset relation of the subgroup \(H_T\) inside the abelian group of
222	  Steps~1--2: both encode
223	  \[
224	    L \sim L' \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
225	      [\, L^{\mathrm{inv}} \otimes L' \,] \in H_T .
226	  \]
227	  Establishing this equivalence of relations is a concrete isomorphism-class
228	  argument over the carrier; it consumes the tensor inverse (Step~1) and the
229	  group law, but not the computational content of the comparison isomorphism.
230	
231	  \emph{Step 4 (transport).} By Step~3 the quotient set underlying
232	  \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) is in canonical bijection with the group quotient
233	  \(\mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T) / H_T\), which carries the canonical
234	  abelian-group structure of a quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup.
235	  Transporting this structure along the bijection of Step~3 endows
236	  \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) with its abelian-group structure, under which the
237	  canonical surjection
238	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \twoheadrightarrow \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) is a
239	  homomorphism with kernel exactly \(H_T\).
240	\end{proof}

204	\subsection{Helper constructions underlying the group law}

207	The relative-Picard carrier of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is the
208	locally-trivial iso-class set modulo the \(H_T\)-coset relation \(\sim\) of
209	\cref{lem:relpic_rel}, in its multiplicative form
210	\[
211	  L \sim L' \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
212	    \exists\, N \text{ locally trivial on } T,\;
213	    L \cong \pi_T^* N \otimes L' .
214	\]
215	Keeping the tensor inverse out of the definition --- it appears only in the
216	symmetry and negation steps --- confines the single upstream project-deferred
217	bridge \(\text{locally trivial} \Rightarrow \text{invertible}\) to those two
218	places. The following project-internal helpers assemble the quotient directly:
219	the relation itself, the iso-class inclusion bridge, its three setoid axioms,
220	the setoid it defines, the well-definedness of addition, and the compatibility
221	of negation. Each is recorded here for the one-to-one Lean correspondence.

231	  \[

334	  trivial (\cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}), \(L \sim L''\).
335	\leanok
336	\end{proof}

338	\begin{lemma}\leanok
339	[Middle-four interchange for \(\otimes\)]
340	  \label{lem:tensor_middle_four}
341	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.tensorMiddleFour}
342	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, def:tensorobjisoofiso}
343	  For arbitrary modules \(A, B, C, D\) on a scheme \(X\) there is a canonical
344	  isomorphism
345	  \[
346	    (A \otimes B) \otimes (C \otimes D)
347	      \;\cong\;
348	    (A \otimes C) \otimes (B \otimes D)
349	  \]
350	  that interchanges the two inner factors. It is the coherence isomorphism used
351	  to show that the objectwise tensor product descends to a well-defined addition
352	  on the relative Picard quotient (\cref{lem:relpic_add_welldef}).
353	\end{lemma}
354	
355	\begin{proof}
356	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, def:tensorobjisoofiso}
357	  \leanok
358	  The isomorphism is assembled entirely from the unconditional associator
359	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} and the braiding \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
360	  applied to a factor through the bifunctoriality of \(\otimes\)
361	  (\cref{def:tensorobjisoofiso}); no coherence pentagon is invoked.
362	  Reassociating and braiding the middle pair,
363	  \[
364	    (A \otimes B) \otimes (C \otimes D)
365	      \cong A \otimes \bigl(B \otimes (C \otimes D)\bigr)
366	      \cong A \otimes \bigl((B \otimes C) \otimes D\bigr)
367	      \cong A \otimes \bigl((C \otimes B) \otimes D\bigr)
368	      \cong A \otimes \bigl(C \otimes (B \otimes D)\bigr)
369	      \cong (A \otimes C) \otimes (B \otimes D) ,
370	  \]
371	  where the first, third-to-last, and last isomorphisms are instances of the
372	  associator, the middle isomorphism braids \(B \otimes C \cong C \otimes B\)
373	  inside the fixed outer factors, and each step past the first is transported
374	  through \(\otimes\) by \cref{def:tensorobjisoofiso}.
375	\end{proof}
376	
377	\begin{lemma}[Addition is well defined on the \(H_T\)-quotient]\leanok
378	  \label{lem:relpic_add_welldef}
379	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relPicRel_add}
380	  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
381	  If \(L_1 \sim L_2\) and \(L_1' \sim L_2'\) then
382	  \(L_1 \otimes L_1' \sim L_2 \otimes L_2'\); hence the descended tensor product
383	  \(+\) is well defined on the quotient
384	  \(\mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T) / {\sim}\).
385	\end{lemma}

387	\begin{proof}
388	  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
389	  Suppose \(L_1 \cong \pi_T^* N \otimes L_2\) and
390	  \(L_1' \cong \pi_T^* N' \otimes L_2'\) with \(N, N'\) locally trivial. By the
391	  middle-four interchange
392	  \((A \otimes B) \otimes (C \otimes D) \cong (A \otimes C) \otimes (B \otimes D)\),
393	  assembled from the associator and the braiding
394	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
395	  \[
396	    L_1 \otimes L_1'
397	      \cong (\pi_T^* N \otimes L_2) \otimes (\pi_T^* N' \otimes L_2')
398	      \cong (\pi_T^* N \otimes \pi_T^* N') \otimes (L_2 \otimes L_2')
399	      \cong \pi_T^*(N \otimes N') \otimes (L_2 \otimes L_2') ,
400	  \]
401	  the last step by the locally-trivial comparison
402	  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv}. As \(N \otimes N'\) is locally
403	  trivial (\cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}),
404	  \(L_1 \otimes L_1' \sim L_2 \otimes L_2'\).
405	\leanok
406	\end{proof}

408	\begin{definition}\leanok
409	[The relative Picard carrier setoid]
410	  \label{lem:relpic_setoid}
411	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relPicSetoid}
412	  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:relpic_setoid_refl, lem:relpic_setoid_symm, lem:relpic_setoid_trans}
413	  The \(H_T\)-coset relation \(\sim\) of \cref{lem:relpic_rel} is an equivalence
414	  relation on \(\mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T)\), by reflexivity
415	  \cref{lem:relpic_setoid_refl}, symmetry \cref{lem:relpic_setoid_symm}, and
416	  transitivity \cref{lem:relpic_setoid_trans}. The resulting setoid presents the
417	  relative Picard quotient \(\Pic(C \times_k T) / H_T\), distinct from the
418	  absolute iso-class setoid \(\mathtt{RelPicPresheaf.preimage\_subgroup}\).
419	\end{definition}

422	[Uniqueness of the tensor inverse up to isomorphism]
423	  \label{lem:tensor_inverse_unique}
424	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.pInverseUnique}
425	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, def:tensorobjisoofiso}
426	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 0B8K (Lemma, \S 17.25 ``Invertible modules'',
427	  % Modules on Ringed Spaces) (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4066-L4079).

429	  % "\begin{lemma}
430	  % \label{lemma-invertible}
431	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space. Let $\mathcal{L}$
432	  % be an $\mathcal{O}_X$-module. Equivalent are
433	  % \begin{enumerate}

435	  % \item there exists an $\mathcal{O}_X$-module $\mathcal{N}$

440	  % $\mathcal{O}_X$-module and the module $\mathcal{N}$ in (2) is isomorphic to
441	  % $\SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X)$.
442	  % \end{lemma}"

444	  two \(\otimes\)-inverses of \(\mathcal{L}\) are each isomorphic to
445	  \(\SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X)\), hence to one
446	  another.}
447	  Let \(M, N, N'\) be modules on a scheme \(X\), and suppose both
448	  \[
449	    M \otimes N \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_X
450	    \qquad\text{and}\qquad
451	    M \otimes N' \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_X .
452	  \]

454	  unique up to a canonical isomorphism.

458	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, def:tensorobjisoofiso}

461	  \(e' : M \otimes N' \cong \mathcal{O}_X\). Using the right unitor, the given
462	  isomorphism \(e'\), the associator, the braiding
463	  \(N \otimes M \cong M \otimes N\) followed by \(e\), and finally the left
464	  unitor,
465	  \[
466	    N \;\cong\; N \otimes \mathcal{O}_X
467	      \;\cong\; N \otimes (M \otimes N')
468	      \;\cong\; (N \otimes M) \otimes N'
469	      \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_X \otimes N'
470	      \;\cong\; N' ,
471	  \]
472	  each objectwise step transported through \(\otimes\) by
473	  \cref{def:tensorobjisoofiso}, with the unitors and associator
474	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} and the braiding
475	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso} supplying the coherence isomorphisms. Hence
476	  \(N \cong N'\).

480	[Negation is compatible with the \(H_T\)-relation]
481	  \label{lem:relpic_rel_neg}

483	  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:pullback_unit_iso}
484	  If \(L \sim M\), then their chosen tensor inverses satisfy \(L^{-1} \sim
485	  M^{-1}\). Hence the descended negation \([L] \mapsto [L^{-1}]\) is well defined
486	  on the quotient \(\mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T) / {\sim}\).
487	\end{lemma}
488	
489	\begin{proof}
490	  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:pullback_unit_iso}
491	  \leanok
492	  Suppose \(L \cong \pi_T^* N \otimes M\) with \(N\) locally trivial, and let
493	  \(N^{-1}\) be its tensor inverse (\cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}),
494	  again locally trivial. Write \(L^{-1}, M^{-1}\) for the chosen tensor inverses
495	  of \(L, M\). We show \(\pi_T^*(N^{-1}) \otimes M^{-1}\) is a tensor inverse of
496	  \(L\): tensoring the hypothesis with \(\pi_T^*(N^{-1}) \otimes M^{-1}\),
497	  reassociating and braiding with
498	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso},
499	  and multiplying the pullbacks through the locally-trivial comparison
500	  isomorphism \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv},
501	  \[
502	    L \otimes \bigl(\pi_T^*(N^{-1}) \otimes M^{-1}\bigr)
503	      \cong \pi_T^*(N \otimes N^{-1}) \otimes (M \otimes M^{-1})
504	      \cong \pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \otimes \mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}
505	      \cong \mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T} ,
506	  \]
507	  using \(M \otimes M^{-1} \cong \mathcal{O}\) and the pullback--unit
508	  isomorphism \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}. By uniqueness of tensor inverses,
509	  \(L^{-1} \cong \pi_T^*(N^{-1}) \otimes M^{-1}\), i.e.\ \(L^{-1} \sim M^{-1}\).
510	\end{proof}

The realized carrier of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is the
locally-trivial iso-class set modulo the \(H_T\)-coset relation \(\sim\), in
its multiplicative form
\[
  L \sim L' \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
    \exists\, N \text{ locally trivial on } T,\;
    L \cong \pi_T^* N \otimes L' .
\]
Keeping the tensor inverse out of the definition --- it appears only in the
symmetry and negation steps --- confines the bridge
\(\text{locally trivial} \Rightarrow \text{invertible}\) to those two
places. The following helpers assemble the quotient directly: the relation
itself, the iso-class inclusion bridge, its three setoid axioms, the setoid it
defines, the middle-four interchange, the well-definedness of addition, and
the compatibility of negation. Each is recorded here for the one-to-one Lean
correspondence.

\begin{definition}\leanok
[The \(H_T\)-coset relation on locally-trivial bundles]
  \label{lem:relpic_rel}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relPicRel}
  \uses{def:line_bundle_on_product, def:pullback_along_projection}
  On the locally-trivial carrier \(\mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T)\) of
  \cref{def:line_bundle_on_product}, define, for locally-trivial \(L, L'\) on
  \(C \times_k T\), the relation
  \[
    L \sim L' \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
      \exists\, N \text{ locally trivial on } T,\;
      L \;\cong\; \pi_T^* N \otimes L' ,
  \]
  where \(\pi_T^* N = \mathtt{pullbackAlongProjection}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\,N\) is the
  pullback of \cref{def:pullback_along_projection}. This \emph{multiplicative
  form} is the left-coset relation of \(H_T := \pi_T^*\Pic(T)\): it holds exactly
  when \([L]\) and \([L']\) differ by an element of \(H_T\) (equivalently
  \(L \otimes L'^{-1} \cong \pi_T^* N\)). It is coarser than the absolute
  iso-class relation \(\mathtt{RelPicPresheaf.preimage\_subgroup}\).
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Iso-class inclusion into the \(H_T\)-coset relation]
  \label{lem:relpic_rel_of_iso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relPicRel_of_iso}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, def:pullback_along_projection, lem:pullback_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  If \(L\) and \(L'\) have isomorphic underlying bundles, then \(L \sim L'\); in
  particular \(\sim\) is coarser than the absolute iso-class relation, which is
  what lets the objectwise coherence isomorphisms descend to the quotient.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:pullback_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  \leanok
  Take \(N = \mathcal{O}_T\). Then
  \[
    \pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \otimes L'
      \cong \mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T} \otimes L'
      \cong L' \cong L ,
  \]
  the first isomorphism by the pullback--unit isomorphism
  \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}, the second by the left unitor
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}, and the last by hypothesis. Hence
  \(L \cong \pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \otimes L'\), so \(L \sim L'\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Reflexivity of the \(H_T\)-relation]\leanok
  \label{lem:relpic_setoid_refl}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relPicRel_refl}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:relpic_rel_of_iso}
  For every locally-trivial \(L\) on \(C \times_k T\) one has \(L \sim L\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel_of_iso}
  Immediate from \cref{lem:relpic_rel_of_iso} applied to the identity
  isomorphism \(L \cong L\). Concretely, taking \(N = \mathcal{O}_T\) gives
  \(\pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \otimes L \cong \mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T} \otimes L
  \cong L\), so \(L \sim L\).
\leanok
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Symmetry of the \(H_T\)-relation]\leanok
  \label{lem:relpic_setoid_symm}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relPicRel_symm}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:pullback_unit_iso}
  If \(L \sim L'\) then \(L' \sim L\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:pullback_unit_iso}
  Suppose \(L \cong \pi_T^* N \otimes L'\) with \(N\) locally trivial, and let
  \(N^{-1}\) be its tensor inverse on \(T\)
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}), again locally trivial. Tensoring
  the hypothesis by \(\pi_T^*(N^{-1})\) and multiplying the pullbacks through
  the locally-trivial comparison isomorphism
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv},
  \[
    \pi_T^*(N^{-1}) \otimes L
      \cong \pi_T^*(N^{-1}) \otimes \pi_T^* N \otimes L'
      \cong \pi_T^*(N^{-1} \otimes N) \otimes L'
      \cong \pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \otimes L'
      \cong L' ,
  \]
  after reassociating and braiding with
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  and applying the pullback--unit isomorphism \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}.
  Hence \(L' \cong \pi_T^*(N^{-1}) \otimes L\) with \(N^{-1}\) locally trivial,
  so \(L' \sim L\).
\leanok
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Transitivity of the \(H_T\)-relation]\leanok
  \label{lem:relpic_setoid_trans}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relPicRel_trans}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
  If \(L \sim L'\) and \(L' \sim L''\) then \(L \sim L''\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
  Suppose \(L \cong \pi_T^* N \otimes L'\) and \(L' \cong \pi_T^* N' \otimes L''\)
  with \(N, N'\) locally trivial. Substituting and reassociating with the
  associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso},
  \[
    L \cong \pi_T^* N \otimes (\pi_T^* N' \otimes L'')
      \cong (\pi_T^* N \otimes \pi_T^* N') \otimes L''
      \cong \pi_T^*(N \otimes N') \otimes L'' ,
  \]
  the last isomorphism by the locally-trivial comparison
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv}. Since \(N \otimes N'\) is locally
  trivial (\cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}), \(L \sim L''\).
\leanok
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Middle-four interchange for \(\otimes\)]
  \label{lem:tensor_middle_four}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.tensorMiddleFour}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, def:tensorobjisoofiso}
  For arbitrary modules \(A, B, C, D\) on a scheme \(X\) there is a canonical
  isomorphism
  \[
    (A \otimes B) \otimes (C \otimes D)
      \;\cong\;
    (A \otimes C) \otimes (B \otimes D)
  \]
  that interchanges the two inner factors. It is the coherence isomorphism used
  to show that the objectwise tensor product descends to a well-defined addition
  on the relative Picard quotient (\cref{lem:relpic_add_welldef}).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, def:tensorobjisoofiso}
  \leanok
  The isomorphism is assembled entirely from the unconditional associator
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} and the braiding \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
  applied to a factor through the bifunctoriality of \(\otimes\)
  (\cref{def:tensorobjisoofiso}); no coherence pentagon is invoked.
  Reassociating and braiding the middle pair,
  \[
    (A \otimes B) \otimes (C \otimes D)
      \cong A \otimes \bigl(B \otimes (C \otimes D)\bigr)
      \cong A \otimes \bigl((B \otimes C) \otimes D\bigr)
      \cong A \otimes \bigl((C \otimes B) \otimes D\bigr)
      \cong A \otimes \bigl(C \otimes (B \otimes D)\bigr)
      \cong (A \otimes C) \otimes (B \otimes D) ,
  \]
  where the first, third-to-last, and last isomorphisms are instances of the
  associator, the middle isomorphism braids \(B \otimes C \cong C \otimes B\)
  inside the fixed outer factors, and each step past the first is transported
  through \(\otimes\) by \cref{def:tensorobjisoofiso}.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Addition is well defined on the \(H_T\)-quotient]\leanok
  \label{lem:relpic_add_welldef}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relPicRel_add}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:tensor_middle_four, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
  If \(L_1 \sim L_2\) and \(L_1' \sim L_2'\) then
  \(L_1 \otimes L_1' \sim L_2 \otimes L_2'\); hence the descended tensor product
  \(+\) is well defined on the quotient
  \(\mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T) / {\sim}\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:tensor_middle_four, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
  Suppose \(L_1 \cong \pi_T^* N \otimes L_2\) and
  \(L_1' \cong \pi_T^* N' \otimes L_2'\) with \(N, N'\) locally trivial. By the
  middle-four interchange
  \((A \otimes B) \otimes (C \otimes D) \cong (A \otimes C) \otimes (B \otimes D)\)
  of \cref{lem:tensor_middle_four}, assembled from the associator and the
  braiding \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
  \[
    L_1 \otimes L_1'
      \cong (\pi_T^* N \otimes L_2) \otimes (\pi_T^* N' \otimes L_2')
      \cong (\pi_T^* N \otimes \pi_T^* N') \otimes (L_2 \otimes L_2')
      \cong \pi_T^*(N \otimes N') \otimes (L_2 \otimes L_2') ,
  \]
  the last step by the locally-trivial comparison
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv}. As \(N \otimes N'\) is locally
  trivial (\cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}),
  \(L_1 \otimes L_1' \sim L_2 \otimes L_2'\).
\leanok
\end{proof}

\begin{definition}\leanok
[The relative Picard carrier setoid]
  \label{lem:relpic_setoid}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relPicSetoid}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:relpic_setoid_refl, lem:relpic_setoid_symm, lem:relpic_setoid_trans}
  The \(H_T\)-coset relation \(\sim\) of \cref{lem:relpic_rel} is an equivalence
  relation on \(\mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T)\), by reflexivity
  \cref{lem:relpic_setoid_refl}, symmetry \cref{lem:relpic_setoid_symm}, and
  transitivity \cref{lem:relpic_setoid_trans}. The resulting setoid presents the
  relative Picard quotient \(\Pic(C \times_k T) / H_T\), distinct from the
  absolute iso-class setoid \(\mathtt{RelPicPresheaf.preimage\_subgroup}\).
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Negation is compatible with the \(H_T\)-relation]
  \label{lem:relpic_rel_neg}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relPicRel_neg}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:pullback_unit_iso}

  M^{-1}\). Hence the descended negation \([L] \mapsto [L^{-1}]\) is well defined
  on the quotient \(\mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T) / {\sim}\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:pullback_unit_iso}
  \leanok
  Suppose \(L \cong \pi_T^* N \otimes M\) with \(N\) locally trivial, and let
  \(N^{-1}\) be its tensor inverse (\cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}),
  again locally trivial. Write \(L^{-1}, M^{-1}\) for the chosen tensor inverses
  of \(L, M\). We show \(\pi_T^*(N^{-1}) \otimes M^{-1}\) is a tensor inverse of
  \(L\): tensoring the hypothesis with \(\pi_T^*(N^{-1}) \otimes M^{-1}\),
  reassociating and braiding with
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso},
  and multiplying the pullbacks through the locally-trivial comparison
  isomorphism \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv},
  \[
    L \otimes \bigl(\pi_T^*(N^{-1}) \otimes M^{-1}\bigr)
      \cong \pi_T^*(N \otimes N^{-1}) \otimes (M \otimes M^{-1})
      \cong \pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \otimes \mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}
      \cong \mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T} ,
  \]

  isomorphism \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}. By uniqueness of tensor inverses
  (\cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_inverse_unique}),
  \(L^{-1} \cong \pi_T^*(N^{-1}) \otimes M^{-1}\), i.e.\ \(L^{-1} \sim M^{-1}\).
\end{proof}

The realized carrier of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is the
locally-trivial iso-class set modulo the \(H_T\)-coset relation \(\sim\), in
its multiplicative form
\[
  L \sim L' \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
    \exists\, N \text{ locally trivial on } T,\;
    L \cong \pi_T^* N \otimes L' .
\]
Keeping the tensor inverse out of the definition --- it appears only in the
symmetry and negation steps --- confines the bridge
\(\text{locally trivial} \Rightarrow \text{invertible}\) to those two
places. The following helpers assemble the quotient directly: the relation
itself, the iso-class inclusion bridge, its three setoid axioms, the setoid it
defines, the middle-four interchange, the well-definedness of addition, and
the compatibility of negation. Each is recorded here for the one-to-one Lean
correspondence.

\begin{definition}\leanok
[The \(H_T\)-coset relation on locally-trivial bundles]
  \label{lem:relpic_rel}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relPicRel}
  \uses{def:line_bundle_on_product, def:pullback_along_projection}
  On the locally-trivial carrier \(\mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T)\) of
  \cref{def:line_bundle_on_product}, define, for locally-trivial \(L, L'\) on
  \(C \times_k T\), the relation
  \[
    L \sim L' \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
      \exists\, N \text{ locally trivial on } T,\;
      L \;\cong\; \pi_T^* N \otimes L' ,
  \]
  where \(\pi_T^* N = \mathtt{pullbackAlongProjection}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\,N\) is the
  pullback of \cref{def:pullback_along_projection}. This \emph{multiplicative
  form} is the left-coset relation of \(H_T := \pi_T^*\Pic(T)\): it holds exactly
  when \([L]\) and \([L']\) differ by an element of \(H_T\) (equivalently
  \(L \otimes L'^{-1} \cong \pi_T^* N\)). It is coarser than the absolute
  iso-class relation \(\mathtt{RelPicPresheaf.preimage\_subgroup}\).
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Iso-class inclusion into the \(H_T\)-coset relation]
  \label{lem:relpic_rel_of_iso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relPicRel_of_iso}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, def:pullback_along_projection, lem:pullback_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  If \(L\) and \(L'\) have isomorphic underlying bundles, then \(L \sim L'\); in
  particular \(\sim\) is coarser than the absolute iso-class relation, which is
  what lets the objectwise coherence isomorphisms descend to the quotient.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:pullback_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  \leanok
  Take \(N = \mathcal{O}_T\). Then
  \[
    \pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \otimes L'
      \cong \mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T} \otimes L'
      \cong L' \cong L ,
  \]
  the first isomorphism by the pullback--unit isomorphism
  \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}, the second by the left unitor
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}, and the last by hypothesis. Hence
  \(L \cong \pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \otimes L'\), so \(L \sim L'\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Reflexivity of the \(H_T\)-relation]\leanok
  \label{lem:relpic_setoid_refl}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relPicRel_refl}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:relpic_rel_of_iso}
  For every locally-trivial \(L\) on \(C \times_k T\) one has \(L \sim L\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel_of_iso}
  Immediate from \cref{lem:relpic_rel_of_iso} applied to the identity
  isomorphism \(L \cong L\). Concretely, taking \(N = \mathcal{O}_T\) gives
  \(\pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \otimes L \cong \mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T} \otimes L
  \cong L\), so \(L \sim L\).
\leanok
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Symmetry of the \(H_T\)-relation]\leanok
  \label{lem:relpic_setoid_symm}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relPicRel_symm}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:pullback_unit_iso}
  If \(L \sim L'\) then \(L' \sim L\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:pullback_unit_iso}
  Suppose \(L \cong \pi_T^* N \otimes L'\) with \(N\) locally trivial, and let
  \(N^{-1}\) be its tensor inverse on \(T\)
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}), again locally trivial. Tensoring
  the hypothesis by \(\pi_T^*(N^{-1})\) and multiplying the pullbacks through
  the locally-trivial comparison isomorphism
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv},
  \[
    \pi_T^*(N^{-1}) \otimes L
      \cong \pi_T^*(N^{-1}) \otimes \pi_T^* N \otimes L'
      \cong \pi_T^*(N^{-1} \otimes N) \otimes L'
      \cong \pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \otimes L'
      \cong L' ,
  \]
  after reassociating and braiding with
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  and applying the pullback--unit isomorphism \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}.
  Hence \(L' \cong \pi_T^*(N^{-1}) \otimes L\) with \(N^{-1}\) locally trivial,
  so \(L' \sim L\).
\leanok
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Transitivity of the \(H_T\)-relation]\leanok
  \label{lem:relpic_setoid_trans}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relPicRel_trans}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
  If \(L \sim L'\) and \(L' \sim L''\) then \(L \sim L''\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
  Suppose \(L \cong \pi_T^* N \otimes L'\) and \(L' \cong \pi_T^* N' \otimes L''\)
  with \(N, N'\) locally trivial. Substituting and reassociating with the
  associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso},
  \[
    L \cong \pi_T^* N \otimes (\pi_T^* N' \otimes L'')
      \cong (\pi_T^* N \otimes \pi_T^* N') \otimes L''
      \cong \pi_T^*(N \otimes N') \otimes L'' ,
  \]
  the last isomorphism by the locally-trivial comparison
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv}. Since \(N \otimes N'\) is locally
  trivial (\cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}), \(L \sim L''\).
\leanok
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Middle-four interchange for \(\otimes\)]
  \label{lem:tensor_middle_four}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.tensorMiddleFour}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, def:tensorobjisoofiso}
  For arbitrary modules \(A, B, C, D\) on a scheme \(X\) there is a canonical
  isomorphism
  \[
    (A \otimes B) \otimes (C \otimes D)
      \;\cong\;
    (A \otimes C) \otimes (B \otimes D)
  \]
  that interchanges the two inner factors. It is the coherence isomorphism used
  to show that the objectwise tensor product descends to a well-defined addition
  on the relative Picard quotient (\cref{lem:relpic_add_welldef}).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, def:tensorobjisoofiso}
  \leanok
  The isomorphism is assembled entirely from the unconditional associator
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} and the braiding \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
  applied to a factor through the bifunctoriality of \(\otimes\)
  (\cref{def:tensorobjisoofiso}); no coherence pentagon is invoked.
  Reassociating and braiding the middle pair,
  \[
    (A \otimes B) \otimes (C \otimes D)
      \cong A \otimes \bigl(B \otimes (C \otimes D)\bigr)
      \cong A \otimes \bigl((B \otimes C) \otimes D\bigr)
      \cong A \otimes \bigl((C \otimes B) \otimes D\bigr)
      \cong A \otimes \bigl(C \otimes (B \otimes D)\bigr)
      \cong (A \otimes C) \otimes (B \otimes D) ,
  \]
  where the first, third-to-last, and last isomorphisms are instances of the
  associator, the middle isomorphism braids \(B \otimes C \cong C \otimes B\)
  inside the fixed outer factors, and each step past the first is transported
  through \(\otimes\) by \cref{def:tensorobjisoofiso}.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Addition is well defined on the \(H_T\)-quotient]\leanok
  \label{lem:relpic_add_welldef}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relPicRel_add}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:tensor_middle_four, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
  If \(L_1 \sim L_2\) and \(L_1' \sim L_2'\) then
  \(L_1 \otimes L_1' \sim L_2 \otimes L_2'\); hence the descended tensor product
  \(+\) is well defined on the quotient
  \(\mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T) / {\sim}\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:tensor_middle_four, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
  Suppose \(L_1 \cong \pi_T^* N \otimes L_2\) and
  \(L_1' \cong \pi_T^* N' \otimes L_2'\) with \(N, N'\) locally trivial. By the
  middle-four interchange
  \((A \otimes B) \otimes (C \otimes D) \cong (A \otimes C) \otimes (B \otimes D)\)
  of \cref{lem:tensor_middle_four}, assembled from the associator and the
  braiding \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
  \[
    L_1 \otimes L_1'
      \cong (\pi_T^* N \otimes L_2) \otimes (\pi_T^* N' \otimes L_2')
      \cong (\pi_T^* N \otimes \pi_T^* N') \otimes (L_2 \otimes L_2')
      \cong \pi_T^*(N \otimes N') \otimes (L_2 \otimes L_2') ,
  \]
  the last step by the locally-trivial comparison
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv}. As \(N \otimes N'\) is locally
  trivial (\cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}),
  \(L_1 \otimes L_1' \sim L_2 \otimes L_2'\).
\leanok
\end{proof}

\begin{definition}\leanok
[The relative Picard carrier setoid]
  \label{lem:relpic_setoid}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relPicSetoid}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:relpic_setoid_refl, lem:relpic_setoid_symm, lem:relpic_setoid_trans}
  The \(H_T\)-coset relation \(\sim\) of \cref{lem:relpic_rel} is an equivalence
  relation on \(\mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T)\), by reflexivity
  \cref{lem:relpic_setoid_refl}, symmetry \cref{lem:relpic_setoid_symm}, and
  transitivity \cref{lem:relpic_setoid_trans}. The resulting setoid presents the
  relative Picard quotient \(\Pic(C \times_k T) / H_T\), distinct from the
  absolute iso-class setoid \(\mathtt{RelPicPresheaf.preimage\_subgroup}\).
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Negation is compatible with the \(H_T\)-relation]
  \label{lem:relpic_rel_neg}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relPicRel_neg}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:pullback_unit_iso, lem:rel_pic_sharp_inverse_unique}
  If \(L \sim M\), then their chosen tensor inverses satisfy \(L^{-1} \sim
  M^{-1}\). Hence the descended negation \([L] \mapsto [L^{-1}]\) is well defined
  on the quotient \(\mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T) / {\sim}\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:relpic_rel, lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv, lem:pullback_unit_iso, lem:rel_pic_sharp_inverse_unique}
  \leanok
  Suppose \(L \cong \pi_T^* N \otimes M\) with \(N\) locally trivial, and let
  \(N^{-1}\) be its tensor inverse (\cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}),
  again locally trivial. Write \(L^{-1}, M^{-1}\) for the chosen tensor inverses
  of \(L, M\). We show \(\pi_T^*(N^{-1}) \otimes M^{-1}\) is a tensor inverse of
  \(L\): tensoring the hypothesis with \(\pi_T^*(N^{-1}) \otimes M^{-1}\),
  reassociating and braiding with
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso},
  and multiplying the pullbacks through the locally-trivial comparison
  isomorphism \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv},
  \[
    L \otimes \bigl(\pi_T^*(N^{-1}) \otimes M^{-1}\bigr)
      \cong \pi_T^*(N \otimes N^{-1}) \otimes (M \otimes M^{-1})
      \cong \pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \otimes \mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}
      \cong \mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T} ,
  \]
  using \(M \otimes M^{-1} \cong \mathcal{O}\) and the pullback--unit
  isomorphism \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}. By uniqueness of tensor inverses
  (\cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_inverse_unique}),
  \(L^{-1} \cong \pi_T^*(N^{-1}) \otimes M^{-1}\), i.e.\ \(L^{-1} \sim M^{-1}\).
\end{proof}

---
name: leandag
description: Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) ‚ÄĒ declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects ‚ÄĒ via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
---

The DAG links blueprint nodes by `\uses` edges. An edge `source -> target` means
*target depends on source*. The engine records each node's `\lean` link,
`proved`/`leanok` status, effort metrics, and Lean source metadata.

## Keep It Fresh

- Run the project build after Lean/blueprint edits so generated Lean artifacts and
  imported libraries are current.
- Run `horizon blueprint` after blueprint or Lean declaration changes; it writes
  `.archon-horizon/blueprints/<project>.json` for the dashboard and cached DAG
  consumers.
- Use `horizon leandag` for live inspection when deciding what to prove next.

## `horizon leandag` (preferred)

- `horizon leandag` ‚ÄĒ per-project summary: nodes, edges, proved count, and any
  isolated nodes (no edge in or out ‚ÄĒ usually a blueprint bug to fix).
- `horizon leandag -p <project> --node <label>` ‚ÄĒ one node's direct deps, full
  transitive cone, and reverse deps.
- `horizon leandag -p a -p b --union` ‚ÄĒ merge projects by declaration/node id.
  Same-id nodes are combined; divergent non-signature metadata is tolerated, but
  a Lean signature mismatch is an error to investigate.
- `horizon leandag -p a -p b --intersect` ‚ÄĒ only shared declaration/node ids,
  with the same signature check.
- Add `--json` for machine-readable output.

A node is "ready" when all dependencies are proved. Prefer ready nodes when
choosing what to attempt next.

## Cached JSON

`horizon blueprint` writes each project's DAG to
`.archon-horizon/blueprints/<project>.json` (`nodes`, `edges`, `dangling`) for
the dashboard. Under the leandag engine, `\uses` are resolved at build/cache
time, so `dangling` is empty there; the parser fallback still populates it. To
check for orphan/unresolved nodes, use `horizon leandag` rather than relying only
on `dangling`.

8	/-!
9	# Tangent-space substrate: derivations and the cotangent dual (A.3.iii substrate)
10	
11	Commutative-algebra substrate for the Kleiman ¬ß5 tangent-space theorem
12	`thm:pic0_tangent_space_iso` (`Picard/Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`,
13	`Scheme.Pic0.tangentSpaceIso`): the identification of the Zariski tangent
14	space of a `k`-scheme at a `k`-rational point with the dual of the cotangent
15	space `m/m¬≤`, expressed at the level of the local ring at the point.
16	
17	Throughout, `R` is a local `k`-algebra. The `k`-rational-point hypothesis is
18	`Function.Bijective (algebraMap k (ResidueField R))` ‚ÄĒ the structure map to
19	the residue field is an isomorphism.
20	
21	## Main declarations
22	
23	- `AlgebraicGeometry.maximalIdeal_smul_residueField` ‚ÄĒ elements of the
24	  maximal ideal act by zero on the residue field.
25	- `AlgebraicGeometry.derivationCotangentDual` ‚ÄĒ a `k`-derivation
26	  `R ‚Üí ResidueField R` kills `m¬≤`, hence descends to an `R`-linear map
27	  `CotangentSpace R ‚Üí‚āó[R] ResidueField R` on `m/m¬≤`.
28	- `AlgebraicGeometry.derivationEquivCotangentDual` ‚ÄĒ for a `k`-rational
29	  local `k`-algebra, descent is an additive equivalence
30	  `Derivation k R (ResidueField R) ‚ČÉ+ (CotangentSpace R ‚Üí‚āó[R] ResidueField R)`,
31	  with inverse built from the canonical splitting `R = k ‚äē m`.
32	- `AlgebraicGeometry.localDualNumberHomEquivDerivation` ‚ÄĒ local `k`-algebra
33	  maps `R ‚Üí k[őĶ]` are `k`-derivations `R ‚Üí ResidueField R` (`őĶ`-coefficient).
34	- `AlgebraicGeometry.localDualNumberHomEquivCotangentSpaceDual` ‚ÄĒ the
35	  capstone: dual-number points of the closed point form the `őļ`-linear dual
36	  of the cotangent space, `Module.Dual (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R)`.
37	
38	This is the "tangent space via dual numbers" paragraph of Kleiman ¬ß5
39	Thm. 5.11: points of a scheme over the dual numbers lifting a `k`-rational
40	point `e` correspond to local `k`-algebra maps `ūĚí™_e ‚Üí k[őĶ]`, i.e. to
41	`k`-derivations `ūĚí™_e ‚Üí k`, i.e. by this file to `Hom(m_e/m_e¬≤, k)`.
42	
43	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_Pic0AbelianVariety.tex`,
44	¬ß `sec:pic0_tangent_space` (`lem:tangent_derivation_cotangent_dual` and
45	friends), feeding `thm:pic0_tangent_space_iso`.
46	-/

60	/-- Elements of the maximal ideal act by zero on the residue field. -/
61	lemma maximalIdeal_smul_residueField {x : R} (hx : x ‚ąą maximalIdeal R)
62	    (c : ResidueField R) : x ‚ÄĘ c = 0 := by
63	  rw [Algebra.smul_def]
64	  have hx0 : algebraMap R (ResidueField R) x = 0 :=
65	    Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem.mpr hx
66	  rw [hx0, zero_mul]
67

72	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] {R : Type v} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
73	  [Algebra k R]

75	/-- The restriction of a `k`-derivation `d : R ‚Üí ResidueField R` to the
76	maximal ideal, as an `R`-linear map `m ‚Üí‚āó[R] ResidueField R`. Linearity over
77	`R` (not merely `k`) holds because the second Leibniz term `x ‚ÄĘ d r` dies:
78	`x ‚ąą m` acts by zero on the residue field. -/
79	noncomputable def derivationRestrictMaximalIdeal (d : Derivation k R (ResidueField R)) :
80	    maximalIdeal R ‚Üí‚āó[R] ResidueField R where
81	  toFun x := d (x : R)
82	  map_add' x y := by simp
83	  map_smul' r x := by
84	    have hrx : ((r ‚ÄĘ x : maximalIdeal R) : R) = r * (x : R) := rfl
85	    rw [hrx, Derivation.leibniz, maximalIdeal_smul_residueField x.2 (d r),
86	      add_zero, RingHom.id_apply]
87	
88	@[simp]
89	lemma derivationRestrictMaximalIdeal_apply (d : Derivation k R (ResidueField R))
90	    (x : maximalIdeal R) :
91	    derivationRestrictMaximalIdeal d x = d (x : R) := rfl
92	
93	/-- A `k`-derivation `R ‚Üí ResidueField R` kills `m ‚ÄĘ m`, hence descends to the
94	cotangent space `m/m¬≤`, `R`-linearly. -/
95	noncomputable def derivationCotangentDual (d : Derivation k R (ResidueField R)) :
96	    CotangentSpace R ‚Üí‚āó[R] ResidueField R :=
97	  Submodule.liftQ _ (derivationRestrictMaximalIdeal d) (by
98	    rw [Submodule.smul_le]
99	    intro r hr x _
100	    simp only [LinearMap.mem_ker, LinearMap.map_smul,
101	      derivationRestrictMaximalIdeal_apply]
102	    exact maximalIdeal_smul_residueField hr _)
103	
104	@[simp]
105	lemma derivationCotangentDual_toCotangent (d : Derivation k R (ResidueField R))
106	    (x : maximalIdeal R) :
107	    derivationCotangentDual d ((maximalIdeal R).toCotangent x) = d (x : R) := rfl

113	/-- The `k`-rational-point hypothesis packaged as a ring equivalence
114	`k ‚ČÉ+* ResidueField R`. -/
115	noncomputable def residueFieldEquivOfBijective : k ‚ČÉ+* ResidueField R :=
116	  RingEquiv.ofBijective _ hres

118	@[simp]
119	lemma residueFieldEquivOfBijective_apply (c : k) :
120	    residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres c

123	lemma residue_algebraMap (c : k) :
124	    residue R (algebraMap k R c) = algebraMap k (ResidueField R) c := by
125	  rw [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply k R (ResidueField R) c]
126	  rfl

128	/-- The canonical "constant part" of `r : R` at a `k`-rational point: the
129	image in `R` of the residue class of `r`, along the inverse of the residue
130	isomorphism. Subtracting it lands in the maximal ideal. -/
131	noncomputable def sectOfBijective (r : R) : R :=
132	  algebraMap k R ((residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm (residue R r))
133	
134	lemma residue_sectOfBijective (r : R) :
135	    residue R (sectOfBijective hres r) = residue R r := by
136	  unfold sectOfBijective
137	  rw [residue_algebraMap]
138	  exact (residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).apply_symm_apply _
139	
140	lemma sub_sectOfBijective_mem_maximalIdeal (r : R) :
141	    r - sectOfBijective hres r ‚ąą maximalIdeal R := by
142	  have h : residue R (r - sectOfBijective hres r) = 0 := by
143	    rw [map_sub, residue_sectOfBijective, sub_self]
144	  exact Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem.mp h

146	lemma sectOfBijective_algebraMap (c : k) :
147	    sectOfBijective hres (algebraMap k R c) = algebraMap k R c := by
148	  unfold sectOfBijective
149	  rw [residue_algebraMap]
150	  congr 1
151	  exact (residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm_apply_apply c

153	lemma sectOfBijective_of_mem {x : R} (hx : x ‚ąą maximalIdeal R) :
154	    sectOfBijective hres x = 0 := by
155	  have h0 : residue R x = 0 := Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem.mpr hx
156	  unfold sectOfBijective
157	  rw [h0, map_zero, map_zero]

159	lemma sectOfBijective_add (r s : R) :
160	    sectOfBijective hres (r + s)
161	      = sectOfBijective hres r + sectOfBijective hres s := by
162	  unfold sectOfBijective
163	  rw [map_add, map_add, map_add]
164	
165	lemma sectOfBijective_mul (r s : R) :
166	    sectOfBijective hres (r * s)
167	      = sectOfBijective hres r * sectOfBijective hres s := by
168	  unfold sectOfBijective
169	  rw [map_mul, map_mul, map_mul]
170	
171	lemma sectOfBijective_one : sectOfBijective hres (1 : R) = 1 := by
172	  unfold sectOfBijective
173	  rw [map_one, map_one, map_one]

175	/-- If `r - sect r ‚ąą m`, then `r` and its constant part act identically on the
176	residue field. -/
177	lemma smul_residueField_eq_sectOfBijective_smul (r : R) (c : ResidueField R) :
178	    r ‚ÄĘ c = sectOfBijective hres r ‚ÄĘ c := by
179	  have h : r ‚ÄĘ c - sectOfBijective hres r ‚ÄĘ c = (r - sectOfBijective hres r) ‚ÄĘ c :=
180	    (sub_smul r (sectOfBijective hres r) c).symm
181	  have h0 : (r - sectOfBijective hres r) ‚ÄĘ c = 0 :=
182	    maximalIdeal_smul_residueField (sub_sectOfBijective_mem_maximalIdeal hres r) c
183	  exact sub_eq_zero.mp (h.trans h0)

185	/-- The element of the maximal ideal cut out by `r : R` under the canonical
186	splitting `R = k ‚äē m` at a `k`-rational point. -/
187	noncomputable def maximalIdealPart (r : R) : maximalIdeal R :=
188	  ‚ü®r - sectOfBijective hres r, sub_sectOfBijective_mem_maximalIdeal hres r‚ü©
189	
190	@[simp]
191	lemma maximalIdealPart_coe (r : R) :
192	    (maximalIdealPart hres r : R) = r - sectOfBijective hres r := rfl
193	
194	lemma maximalIdealPart_of_mem {x : R} (hx : x ‚ąą maximalIdeal R) :
195	    maximalIdealPart hres x = ‚ü®x, hx‚ü© := by
196	  ext
197	  rw [maximalIdealPart_coe, sectOfBijective_of_mem hres hx, sub_zero]
198	
199	lemma maximalIdealPart_add (r s : R) :
200	    maximalIdealPart hres (r + s)
201	      = maximalIdealPart hres r + maximalIdealPart hres s := by
202	  ext
203	  simp only [maximalIdealPart_coe, Submodule.coe_add, sectOfBijective_add]
204	  ring

206	/-- The Leibniz decomposition of the maximal-ideal part of a product. The
207	last summand lies in `m¬≤`. -/
208	lemma maximalIdealPart_mul (r s : R) :
209	    maximalIdealPart hres (r * s)
210	      = sectOfBijective hres r ‚ÄĘ maximalIdealPart hres s
211	        + sectOfBijective hres s ‚ÄĘ maximalIdealPart hres r
212	        + ‚ü®(r - sectOfBijective hres r) * (s - sectOfBijective hres s),
213	           Ideal.mul_mem_right _ _ (sub_sectOfBijective_mem_maximalIdeal hres r)‚ü© := by
214	  ext
215	  simp only [maximalIdealPart_coe, Submodule.coe_add, SetLike.val_smul,
216	    smul_eq_mul, sectOfBijective_mul]
217	  ring

219	/-- The inverse construction: an `R`-linear functional on the cotangent space
220	`m/m¬≤` extends to a `k`-derivation `R ‚Üí ResidueField R` via the canonical
221	splitting `R = k ‚äē m` at a `k`-rational point. -/
222	noncomputable def cotangentDualToDerivation
223	    (ŌÜ : CotangentSpace R ‚Üí‚āó[R] ResidueField R) :
224	    Derivation k R (ResidueField R) where
225	  toFun r := ŌÜ ((maximalIdeal R).toCotangent (maximalIdealPart hres r))
226	  map_add' r s := by
227	    rw [maximalIdealPart_add, map_add, map_add]
228	  map_smul' c r := by
229	    have hpart : maximalIdealPart hres (c ‚ÄĘ r)
230	        = algebraMap k R c ‚ÄĘ maximalIdealPart hres r := by
231	      ext
232	      simp only [maximalIdealPart_coe, SetLike.val_smul, smul_eq_mul]
233	      have hsm : (c ‚ÄĘ r : R) = algebraMap k R c * r := Algebra.smul_def c r
234	      rw [hsm, sectOfBijective_mul, sectOfBijective_algebraMap]
235	      ring
236	    rw [hpart, map_smul, map_smul, RingHom.id_apply, algebraMap_smul]
237	  map_one_eq_zero' := by
238	    change ŌÜ ((maximalIdeal R).toCotangent (maximalIdealPart hres (1 : R))) = 0
239	    have h1 : maximalIdealPart hres (1 : R) = 0 := by
240	      ext
241	      rw [maximalIdealPart_coe, sectOfBijective_one, sub_self,
242	        ZeroMemClass.coe_zero]
243	    rw [h1, map_zero, map_zero]
244	  leibniz' r s := by
245	    change ŌÜ ((maximalIdeal R).toCotangent (maximalIdealPart hres (r * s)))
246	        = r ‚ÄĘ ŌÜ ((maximalIdeal R).toCotangent (maximalIdealPart hres s))
247	          + s ‚ÄĘ ŌÜ ((maximalIdeal R).toCotangent (maximalIdealPart hres r))
248	    have hsq : (maximalIdeal R).toCotangent
249	        ‚ü®(r - sectOfBijective hres r) * (s - sectOfBijective hres s),
250	         Ideal.mul_mem_right _ _ (sub_sectOfBijective_mem_maximalIdeal hres r)‚ü©
251	          = 0 := by
252	      rw [Ideal.toCotangent_eq_zero, pow_two]
253	      exact Ideal.mul_mem_mul
254	        (sub_sectOfBijective_mem_maximalIdeal hres r)
255	        (sub_sectOfBijective_mem_maximalIdeal hres s)
256	    rw [maximalIdealPart_mul]
257	    simp only [map_add, map_smul, hsq, add_zero]
258	    rw [‚Üź smul_residueField_eq_sectOfBijective_smul hres r,
259	      ‚Üź smul_residueField_eq_sectOfBijective_smul hres s]

261	@[simp]
262	lemma cotangentDualToDerivation_apply
263	    (ŌÜ : CotangentSpace R ‚Üí‚āó[R] ResidueField R) (r : R) :
264	    cotangentDualToDerivation hres ŌÜ r
265	      = ŌÜ ((maximalIdeal R).toCotangent (maximalIdealPart hres r)) := rfl
